work.name: University of Manchester
work.summary: Undertaking a PhD in Theoretical Chemistry, focusing on the application of sigma-hole interactions in organocatalysis.

awards.title: Best Presentation Prize, Computational Chemistry
awards.awarder: Department of Chemistry PGR Conference, University of Manchester
awards.summary: Awarded Best Presentation Prize in the Computational Chemistry section at the annual Postgraduate Research Conference.

awards.title: PhD Studentship, Application of Sigma-Hole Interactions in Organocatalysis
awards.summary: Funded 3.5-year PhD in Theoretical Chemistry, supervised by Dr. Cristina Trujillo, focusing on the computational study of sigma-hole interactions and their application in organocatalysis.

awards.title: RSC Internship Fund
awards.summary: Funded two-month research internship in the Trujillo Group, University of Manchester, studying sigma-hole interactions in organocatalysis.

publications.name: Synthesis of Macrocyclic Peptides via Photochemical Radical Thiol-yne Reaction
publications.publisher: Journal of the American Chemical Society
publications.summary: A radical-mediated, thiol-yne process for peptide macrocyclization is reported, furnishing vinyl sulfide products under mild conditions. The methodology enables the formation of cyclic peptides compatible with unprotected sequences, generating a disulfide bioisostere to biologically relevant neuropeptides. A novel vinyl sulfide analogue of oxytocin with enhanced potency and redox stability over the parent compound is identified.

publications.name: Neutral Chiral Bidentate Tellurium-Triazoles for Enantioselective Non-Covalent Chalcogen-Bonding Catalysis
publications.summary: Design and application of neutral chiral chalcogen-bond donors based on bidentate tellurium-triazole structures for enantioselective catalysis. Computational analysis guided optimisation of the chiral backbone, leading to enantioselectivities of up to 78% ee in a Reissert-type benchmark reaction.

publications.name: Oxenoid Reactivity Enabled by Targeted Photoactivation of Periodate
publications.summary: A conceptually novel approach to oxene generation through the violet-light photolysis of tetrabutylammonium periodate is reported. DFT calculations reveal an unexpected geometric change upon photoexcitation facilitating intersystem crossing and near-barrierless dissociation of triplet periodate into oxene, enabling epoxidation across a broad substrate scope.

publications.name: Computational Design of Bidentate Hypervalent Iodine Catalysts in Halogen Bond-Mediated Organocatalysis
publications.summary: A systematic DFT-based study of a bidentate hypervalent iodine(III) scaffold for halogen-bond-mediated organocatalysis. Substituent and scaffold effects on binding affinity and activation barriers are evaluated, establishing key computational design principles for sigma-hole-based catalysis.
